page: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. This instance features 12 dedicated vCPUs derived from 6 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz. The CPU subsystem includes 38 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX-512 vector instructions. System memory totals 170 GB of RAM, providing approximately 14.17 GB per core. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU equipped with 80 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. Local storage consists of a single 402 GB NVMe SSD.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance asymmetry between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, and single-threaded compression tasks also fall into the lower performance tier. Multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average range. Conversely, the server achieves top-tier performance in large language model inference. Prompt processing and text generation benchmarks for small, medium, and large models consistently rank in the top 10%, demonstrating the high efficiency of the integrated NVIDIA A100 GPU for transformer-based workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents a specialized hardware profile where resource density is heavily weighted toward GPU acceleration and memory capacity rather than raw CPU compute power. The high ratio of VRAM and system memory relative to the 12 vCPUs makes it a highly targeted option for deep learning, data science, and AI inference. It is specifically suited for LLM inference, machine learning model evaluation, and GPU-accelerated data processing pipelines, while being less optimal for CPU-heavy sequential processing or general-purpose web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for GPU-intensive workloads. It features an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 12 dedicated vCPUs, 170 GB of RAM, and a local 402 GB NVMe SSD. The primary computational driver is a single NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 80 GB of VRAM. While synthetic benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU performance and weaker single-core processing speeds, the server excels in machine learning tasks. Specifically, it achieves top-tier benchmark results in large language model inference for prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes. This hardware configuration is highly optimized for deep learning, AI inference, and data science workloads that require substantial GPU memory and high-bandwidth memory access, rather than general-purpose or CPU-bound sequential computing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 12 vCPUs, 170 GB of RAM, a 402 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 80 GB of VRAM for high-performance AI inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g features 12 vCPUs, 170 GB RAM, a 402 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA A100 GPU with 80 GB VRAM for AI workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform pairing an NVIDIA A100 GPU with high-capacity system memory for top-tier large language model inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU featuring 80 GB of VRAM.
Includes 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 170 GB of system memory.
Features a local 402 GB NVMe SSD for fast temporary storage.
Delivers top-tier performance in large language model inference and prompt processing.
Best suited for deep learning, AI inference, and GPU-accelerated data science.
